(2) Notice must also have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (3) Schedule A does not show any descriptions (source of receipts) other than interest on page 3 of 3. (4) When an accounting is presented, original bank or brokerage statements must be filed to verify the ending balance(s) shown in the accounting. Copies attached to the petition are not sufficient. [LRSF 14.34 D.] See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. (5) The accounting period claimed in paragraph 2 of the petition and in paragraph 1 of the prayer is erroneous. Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by8/9/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF THOMAS FRANCISCO LEGASPI FERIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 08/06/2024 (no earlier date available), as nothing has been filed to address the following threshold issue (e-mailed to counsel on 06/26/2024): The accounting was filed by the petitioners presumably as limited co-conservators of the estate, but neither the appointment order nor the underlying petition specified the powers and duties of the limited co-conservators of the estate. As this is a limited conservatorship case, the specific powers and duties of the limited co-conservators of the estate must be set forth in the petition and order. See Probate Code sections 1821(j)(1)(A) & 1830(b). As such, the petitioners appear to have no authority in their capacity as limited co-conservators of the estate to manage the limited conservatee's Chase bank account (which has been inventoried and appraised as the sole asset of this limited conservatorship estate) or any other finance for that matter, although the Chase account statements attached to the accounting indicate that the limited conservatee and petitioner Lorna Feria continue to be joint account holders, allowing Lorna to manage the account in her individual capacity (as a joint owner). Unless a fully-noticed petition is filed to pray for the specific powers and duties of the limited co-conservators of the estate, the appointment order and letters must be amended to remove the limited conservatorship of the estate, in which case no court accounting will be required. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF WILLIAM COLE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/21/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) An unregistered domestic partner is not kin. If the decedent was not survived by issue or a spouse or registered domestic partner, who were his parents and when did they die? [LRSF 14.18 D.1.] (2) If the decedent was not survived by any kin (no aunts, uncles, or cousins either?), the petitioner must set forth the efforts she made to locate them. [LRSF 14.18 C.1.] (3) The petitioner does not have priority before the Public Administrator. [Probate Code Section 8461.] Either the Public Administrator must petition or decline to serve. (4) Item 8 of the petition does not show the relationship to the decedent. (5) The Waiver of Bond, while arguably not necessary, was incomplete at item 4. (6) This Court does not ordinarily permit funds to be deposited in a blocked account at the outset of estate administration. [LRSF 14.16 M.3.] Deadline for providing courtesy copies to the examiner of any responsive papers is 8/9/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GERALDINE CLARK
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Instructions Regarding Partial Allowance And Partial Rejection Of Creditor?S Claims Filed By Elizabeth Bryant Stewart And David C. Stewart
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Each creditor claim requests reimbursement of fees and costs paid to each claimant's own attorneys. The legal services rendered by the attorneys, both before and after the decedent's death, included those related to the decedent's conservatorship and appointment of the successor trustee of the decedent's trust, the sole devisee. The Court is to determine whether each claim fully or partially qualifies as "liability of the decedent" within the meaning of Probate Code section 9000(a)(1) for which a creditor claim must be filed, rather than post-death expenses of administration. See LRSF 14.28(A)("The Court will not approve a claim that represents obligations of the estate arising after the death of the decedent (except reasonable funeral expenses). Such expenses should be included for approval in the account or in the report on waiver of account.") The Court is also to determine whether each claimant has any cause of action (e.g., quasi-contract, common count, unjust enrichment, etc.) against the decedent personally purporting to form the basis of the claim. See Conservatorship of Brokken (2021) 61 Cal.App.5th 944 ("No conservator was ever appointed and, consequently, appellant never became a conservatee. Thus, there is no "estate of the conservatee" from which to pay attorney fees. Respondents cite no authority suggesting the court may order a non-conservatee to pay the fees."); see also Welborne v. Ryman-Carroll Foundation (2018) 22 Cal.App.5th 719 ("The fact that one person benefits another is not, by itself, sufficient to require restitution. The person receiving the benefit is required to make restitution only if the circumstances are such that, as between the two individuals, it is unjust for the person to retain it".) PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE ILSE LEMKE TRUST DATED JUNE 17, 1991
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Expunge Notice Of Pendency Of Action Lis Pendens
rulings.Rulings: [PART ONE OF FIVE] This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Ross C. Moody. Before the Court is Trustor Ilse Lemke's Motion to Expunge Notice of Pendency of Action (Lis Pendens). Movant Ilse Lemke's Request for Judicial Notice of certain recorded real property records is GRANTED, under Evidence Code section 452 subd. (d) and (h). The Court DENIES Anna Kauffman's request in the alternative to continue hearing on this Motion pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 405.30, to allow her to conduct further discovery on her underlying claim that Ilse Lemke is legally incompetent to act as Trustee of the Ilse Lemke Trust UDT 6/17/91, as amended. Trustor Ilse Lemke's Motion to Expunge Notice of Pendency of Action (Lis Pendens) is GRANTED, and monetary sanctions are awarded, for the reasons discussed below. "At any time after notice of pendency of action has been recorded, any party, or any nonparty with an interest in the real property affected thereby, may apply to the court in which the action is pending to expunge the notice. [ ] Evidence or declarations may be filed with the motion to expunge the notice. [ ] The claimant shall have the burden of proof under Sections 405.31 and 405.32." (Code of Civil Procedure section 405.30.) The court shall order the notice expunged if the court finds either that the pleading on which the notice is based does not contain a real property claim, or that the claimant has not established by a preponderance of the evidence the probable validity of the real property claim. (Code of Civil Procedure sections 405.31, 405.32; see J&A Mash & Barrel, LLC v. Superior Court (2022) 74 Cal. App. 5th 1, 22 ["The only substantive grounds upon which a lis pendens can be expunged is if the underlying action does not contain a real property claim or the real property claim lacks probable validity."].) A claim has "probable validity" where it is more likely than not that the plaintiff will obtain a judgment against the defendant on that claim. (Code of Civil Procedure section 481.190.) 1. The pleading on which the lis pendens is based contains a real property claim. A "real property claim" requires a pleading to (1) identify specific real property that is the subject of the action, and (2) show that a cause or causes of action would affect title to or right to possession of the specific property. (Gale v. Superior Court (2004) 122 CA4th 1388, 1396.) Here, on July 12, 2023, Petitioner Anna Kauffman filed Petition For Order Settling Accounts, Passing Upon Acts Of And Instructing Co-Trustee ("Petition"), in her capacity as co-trustee of the Ilse Lemke Trust UDT 6/17/91, as amended. On November 13, 2023, Anna Kauffman filed First Amendment To Petition For Order Settling Accounts, Passing Upon Act Of An Instructing Co-Trustee To Request Additional Orders Modifying Trust And Removing Trustee ("FAP"). On November 15, 2023, Kauffman caused a Notice of Pendency of Action (Lis Pendens) to be recorded on the real property commonly known as 21 Privateer Drive, Corte Madera, California 94925. The Notice was filed in this matter January 4, 2024. The parties do not dispute that the Petition and First Amended Petition identifies a specific real property that is, in part, the subject of the action. In the initial Petition, Kauffman sought an order under Probate Code section 17200, in part, "adopting her proposed financial plan for Ilse, which includes, among other things, renting the Corte Madera home and using the rental income to support Ilse," rather than selling the property. (Pet. at 3:28-30). In the First Amended Petition, Kauffman presents further argument that the real property should be rented and not sold, so as to avoid capital gains tax liability. [END OF PART ONE OF FIVE]

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE ILSE LEMKE TRUST DATED JUNE 17, 1991
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Notice Of Motion And Motion To Expunge Notice Of Pendency Of Action Lis Pendens (Added For Posting Of Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: [PART FOUR OF FIVE] In support of the Motion, Lemke has sworn under penalty of perjury that she has wanted to sell her Corte Madera property for over two years, but Kauffman has obstructed the sale, and she must sell the property to pay for the costs of this trust litigation and for her other daily expenses. She further states she has instructed co-trustee Karen Riemer of Badger Bank to sell the Corte Madera property as soon as possible "because it is necessary and it is what I want." (Decl. of Lemke at par. 3, 6, 7, 8.) Per the declaration of Riemer filed in support of the Motion, after review of Lemke's Bank of Marin account, she does not have sufficient liquid assets to fund this litigation and to pay for her health, maintenance, shelter, and support; that, in Riemer's professional opinion, sale of the Corte Madera property is necessary to pay for these expenses; and Lemke has told her "she wants to sell her Corte Madera property as she has no intention of returning to California." (Decl. of Riemer at par. 3, 5, 6, 7.) In the Trustor's Supplement to First Amended Objection and Request for Dismissal in Relation to Anna Kauffman's First Amended Petition, etc., filed on May 17, 2024, and the Trustor's First Amended Response, etc. filed January 17, 2024, both verified by Lemke, Lemke objects to Kauffman's claim in the FAP that she lacks legal capacity to manage her property and financial affairs, citing in part to Kauffman's willingness to engage her in an all-day meditation and settlement agreement negotiation in this matter, in December 2023. On the record before the Court, Kauffman has not met her burden of proof on the allegation that Lemke lacked the capacity to execute the February 2024 Third Amendment to Trust, nor rebutted the legal presumption that Lemke possesses the capacity to make decisions and to be responsible for her acts or decisions. b. Kauffman has not carried the burden of proof on the allegation that Lemke executed the February 2024 Trust amendment under the undue influence of another Undue influence, in the context of a testamentary disposition of property by will or trust, is "pressure brought to bear directly on the testamentary act, sufficient to overcome the testator's free will, amounting in effect to coercion destroying the testator's free agency." (Rice v. Clark (2002) 28 Cal.4th 89, 96.) The determination of whether undue influence was exerted to obtain a certain testamentary disposition is, ultimately, a factual inquiry. There is no fixed formula for determining undue influence. (Beckmann v. Beckmann (1959)174 Cal. App. 2d 717, 721.) Under Welfare & Institutions Code section 15610.70 and Probate Code section 86, " 'Undue influence' means excessive persuasion that causes another person to act or refrain from acting by overcoming that person's free will and results in inequity." In making that determination, the following elements must be considered: (1) the vulnerability of the victim; (2) the influencer's apparent authority; (3) the actions or tactics used by the influencer, such as controlling necessaries of life, medication, the victim's interactions with others, access to information, or sleep, use of affection, intimidation, or coercion, initiation of changes in personal or property rights, use of haste or secrecy in effecting those changes; and (4) the equity of the result. Evidence of an inequitable result, without more, is not sufficient to prove undue influence. (Welfare & Institutions Code section 15610.70.) [END OF PART FOUR OF FIVE]

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE ILSE LEMKE TRUST DATED JUNE 17, 1991
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Notice Of Motion And Motion To Expunge Notice Of Pendency Of Action Lis Pendens (Added For Posting Of Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: [PART THREE OF FIVE] As excerpted in the opposition papers, Dr. Dralle stated in his deposition he had not performed any tests on Lemke to determine her ability to make independent decisions regarding finances, and in his professional opinion it would not be appropriate to render an opinion about a person's legal capacity without administering an objective test. (See, Decl. of Harris ISO Opp. at par. 11.) Kauffman bears the burden of proof to rebut the legal presumption of Lemke's capacity to make decisions, specifically her decision to amend her Trust. Neither Dr. Dralle's letter, nor Kauffman's impeachment of it contribute to Kauffman meeting this burden of proof. Attached as Exhibit C to FAP is a document titled "Neurological Evaluation" dated November 15, 2022. This documents lists under the heading "Diagnosis: Mild dementia, consider Alzheimer's or LATE." The document does not express an opinion regarding Lemke's ability to manage her property or financial affairs. Instead, the document states under the "Background Information" heading, "The referral was initially requested by family to answer legal questions about financial/legal capacity. Prior to her appointment, I explained to Monique that this was beyond the scope of this evaluation and provided outside resources for a medicolegal evaluation. Ilse and Monique said they would like to proceed with this clinical evaluation to establish a baseline and understand any cognitive changes and what might help." On its face, this document was not prepared to render an opinion on Lemke's ability to manage her financial affairs. The diagnosis of a mental disorder is, alone, not enough to support a determination that a person lacks the legal capacity to perform a specific act or decision. (See Probate Code section 810(c).) As stated in the Opposition to the Motion, Kauffman also relies on the following as the basis for her claim that Lemke lacks the ability to effectively manage her property or financial affairs: "[Lemke] hiring three different attorneys to request an accounting of a Trust, then filing an objection stating that Anna should not have prepared an accounting; moving forward with the sale of her California home where the sale is unnecessary and would result in an avoidable almost half a million dollar loss that she previously expressed she would like to avoid; removing Anna as co-trustee so that "people would get along" and appointing a fiduciary at as yet unknown costs and despite the fact that Ms. Lemke has trusted Anna as her fiduciary since appointing Anna her financial and health care agent in 1997." (Opp. at par. 18.) On the record before the Court, Kauffman presents no evidence to support the implicit assertion that Lemke lacked the ability to communicate these decisions, or to understand and appreciate their consequences. [END OF PART THREE OF FIVE]

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE ILSE LEMKE TRUST DATED JUNE 17, 1991
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Notice Of Motion And Motion To Expunge Notice Of Pendency Of Action Lis Pendens (Added For Posting Of Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: [PART TWO OF FIVE] In addition to the relief initially sought in the Petition, the FAP seeks an order of this court (1) determining Lemke is legally disabled under the terms of the Trust, due to lack of capacity to substantially manage her financial affairs and resist undue influence, and removing her as a co-trustee, and (2) modifying the Trust to make it irrevocable based on Lemke's legal disability. (FAP at pgs. 6, 11, 13.) 2. On the record before the Court, Kauffman has not established by a preponderance of the evidence the probable validity of the real property claim, as required to maintain lis pendens notice The legislative intent of the lis pendens statutory scheme "was to make the standard on a motion to expunge a notice of lis pendens equivalent to the probable validity standard applicable under the attachment law (Code Civil Procedure section 484.090) [citations]. The burden is on Kauffman to "establish by a preponderance of the evidence the probable validity of the real property claim." That is, Kauffman must make "at least establish a prima facie case. If the defendant makes an appearance, the court must then consider the relative merits of the positions of the respective parties and make a determination of the probable outcome of the litigation." (Howard S. Wright Constr. Co. v. Superior Ct. (2003) 106 Cal. App. 4th 314, 319-20, quoting Cal. Law Revision Comm. coms. to Code Civil Procedure section 481.190, definition of "probable validity" in attachment actions.) Kauffman's claim to the trust real property in her capacity as co-trustee is premised on the claim that Lemke, as settlor and co-trustee, lacks the necessary legal capacity to have validly modified the Trust to remove Kauffman as a co-trustee and appoint a third party fiduciary in her place. Under Art. 4, Sec. 2 of the Trust, "Trustor shall also be deemed to be disabled for purposes of this agreement upon the determination of a court competent [sic] jurisdiction that she is incompetent, incapacitated, or otherwise legally unable to effectively manage her property or financial affairs." A person challenging the validity of a trust instrument on the grounds that the trustor lacked capacity to execute the document or did so under the undue influence of another carries the burden of proof on those allegations. (Doolittle v. Exchange Bank (2015) 241 Cal.App.4th 529, 545.) a. Kauffman has not carried the burden of proof on the allegation that Lemke lacked capacity to execute the February 2024 Trust amendment There is a "rebuttable presumption affecting the burden of proof that all persons have the capacity to make decisions and to be responsible for their acts or decisions." (Probate Code section 810.) The party challenging that presumption bears the burden of proof. A determination that a person lacks "the legal capacity to perform a specific act should be based on evidence of a deficit in one or more of the person's mental functions rather than on a diagnosis of a person's mental or physical disorder." (Probate Code section 810). A person has capacity to make a decision if the person has the ability to communicate the decision and to understand and appreciate "[t]he rights, duties, and responsibilities created by, or affected by the decision[;] [t]he probable consequences for the decisionmaker and, where appropriate, the persons affected by the decision[;] [t]he significant risks, benefits, and reasonable alternatives involved in the decision." (Probate Code section 812). The Opposition refutes a February 5, 2024 opinion letter by Lemke's neighbor Dr. William G. Dralle regarding Lemke's ability to amend her Trust and manage her property and financial affairs, with reference to Dr. Dralle's deposition testimony regarding how he arrived at his opinion and how he came to draft the letter. [END OF PART TWO OF FIVE]

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE ILSE LEMKE TRUST DATED JUNE 17, 1991
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Notice Of Motion And Motion To Expunge Notice Of Pendency Of Action Lis Pendens (Added For Posting Of Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: [PART FIVE OF FIVE] On the record before the Court, Kauffman does not meet her burden of proof on the claim that Lemke amended the Trust in February 2024 to remove Kauffman as a co-trustee and appoint a third party fiduciary in her place as a result of undue influence. In the excerpts of Dr. Dralle's deposition offered in support of the Opposition, Dr. Dralle opined "the main reason" Lemke appointed a third-party fiduciary was to avoid conflict between her daughters. (Decl. of Harris ISO Opp. at par. 16.) While Dr. Dralle went on to testify he believed Lemke appointed a third party fiduciary at the suggestion of daughter Monique (ibid.), there is no showing on the record before the Court how Lemke came to choose Karen Riemer of Badger Bank as the new co-trustee, and no showing that the appointment was the result of undue influence. Per the declaration of Riemer filed in support of the Opposition, she is an attorney licensed to practice in the state of Wisconsin, and employed as a Senior Trust Officer at Badger Bank in Wisconsin. (Decl. of Riemer at par. 1, 2.) These credentials plainly qualify her to act as co-trustee. And the selection of a trust officer and attorney (with the ethical obligations inherent in those roles) is not suggestive of undue influence. 3. Sanctions Under Code of Civil Procedure section 405.38, "The court shall direct that the party prevailing on any motion under this chapter be awarded the reasonable attorney's fees and costs of making or opposing the motion unless the court finds that the other party acted with substantial justification or that other circumstances make the imposition of attorney's fees and costs unjust." Sanctions are awarded pursuant to Civil Procedure section 405.38. On the record currently before the court, there is no showing that Kauffman acted with substantial justification in opposing this Motion, or that other circumstances make the imposition of the sanction unjust. Petitioner Anna Kauffman is ordered to pay Movant Ilse Lemke a total of two thousand five hundred and seventy dollars ($2,570) in monetary sanctions, within 10 days of service of Notice of Entry of Order. Movant shall prepare and lodge with the court clerk in Department 204 a hard-copy proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling prior to the hearing. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765 and passcode 514879. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765# and passcode 514879#. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.
